# Binomial regression with a logit link

When the response is **yes/no** — species present or absent, survived or not — we
model the **probability** of a "yes". Probabilities live in $[0, 1]$, so again we
need a link that keeps predictions in range.

## The logit link

$$
y_i \sim \text{Bernoulli}(p_i), \qquad
\text{logit}(p_i) = \log\!\frac{p_i}{1 - p_i} = \alpha + \beta\,x_i
$$

The logit maps a probability to the whole real line, so the linear predictor can
roam freely and we transform back with the inverse logit.

In FANGS:

```
model {
  for (i in 1:N) {
    present[i] ~ dbern(p[i])
    logit(p[i]) <- alpha + beta * x[i]
  }
  alpha ~ dnorm(0, 10)
  beta  ~ dnorm(0, 10)
}
```

## Interpreting on the logit scale

A slope $\beta$ is a change in **log-odds** per unit $x$; $e^{\beta}$ is an
**odds ratio**. Positive $\beta$ → presence becomes more likely as $x$ increases.
To get a probability for a given $x$, push the linear predictor through the
inverse logit.

## Your task

Fit the logistic model to `presence.csv` and check your posterior means for
$\alpha$ and $\beta$ (logit scale).
